California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Thomas J. Harron 
City Attorney 
City of Chula vista 
276 Fourth Avenue 
Chula Vista, CA 
92010 
Dear Mr. Harron: 
December 16, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-86-3l6 
You have requested advice concerning Chula Vista City 
Councilmember David Malcolm's duties under the conflict of 
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1I 
Since you 
have not provided us with all of the material facts relevant to 
any particular decision, we consider your letter to be a 
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation l8329(c) 
(copy enclosed) . 
You have asked for advice regarding whether Councilmember 
Malcolm may participate in decisions which will affect a 
general partner in a business in which Councilmember Malcolm is 
a limited partner. 
You have indicated that Councilmember 
Malcolm is involved in several different limited partnerships. 
This issue was thoroughly dealt with in the Commission's 
Nord Opinion, 8 FPPC Opinions 6 (No. 83-004, Oct. 4, 1983) 
(copy enclosed). 
The opinion should be reviewed carefully in 
order to determine Mr. 
Malcolm's obligations. 
It should be 
noted that since the time Nord was issued, the 
11 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California 
Administrative Code Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California 
Administrative Code. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916)322,5660 


Thomas J. Harron 
December 16, 1986 
Page 2 
Commission has adopted Regulation 18236 (copy enclosed) which 
defines the term "otherwise related business entity." 
If you should have any questions, please contact me at 
(916) 322-5901.

Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

Counsel, Legal Division

Re: 
Your Request for Advice

If in the future

Dear Mr. Harron: 
July 25, 1986 
Re: 
86-241 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received on July 22, 1986 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
Very truly yours,

Dear Mr. McLean: 
JUl22 8 19 AN tB6 
July 
8, 1986 
This letter is a 
follow-up to our conversation on Wednesday, 
July 16, 1986 with regard to a q ues t ion that Counc i Iman David 
l'lalcolm brought up on conflict of interest. 
It involves the 
following fact situation: 
Councilman Malcolm and a partner have made an offer to 
purchase a piece of property. 
The purchase amount is 
presently established. 
The purchase is contingent upon 
the 
property 
receiving 
necessary 
approvals 
from 
appropriate governmental agencies 
(not 
including the 
City of Chula Vista). 
It is estimated that it will 
take at least two years to get the approvals necessary 
to develop the proper 
Councilman Malcolm and his 
partner are equal partners. 
ay 
Councilman 
Malcolm 
vote on projects propo 
by his 
rtner unrelat 
to 
this project? 
In our phone conversation, you advised me that the Commission 
discussed this issue in November and changed its position from 
earlier opinions, so that now Councilman Malcolm may 
vote on 
unrelated projects. 
When I informed Councilman Malcolm of this 
advice, he asked me to request an advice letter from you. 
It is 
my understanding that you have not prepared any advice let ters 
since the November meeting, 
that this one would be the first 
one to 
ress this subj 
r 
t 
e 
AVENUE/CHULA 
92010/(619) 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Thomas J. Harron 
Chula Vista City Attorney 
276 Fourth Avenue 
Chula Vista, CA 
92010 
Dear Mr. Harron: 
November 20, 1986 
Re: 
86-316 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on November 17, 1986, by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
Commission Regulation 18329(b) (2) (copy 
enclosed) requires a request for formal written advice to 
include a specific statement that you have been authorized to 
request the advice by the person whose duties under the Act are 
in question. 
Please send us a supplemental letter stating that 
you are so authorized by the official on whose behalf you have 
requested advice. 
Please also include in your letter the 
mailing address of the official. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days after we receive your suppiemental 
letter. 
You also should be aware that your letter and our 
response are public records which may be disclosed to the 
public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
If you 
have any questions about your advice request, you may contact 
John G. McLean, an attorney in the Legal Division, directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
Very truly yours, 
bL~ ~H1 (~1 ~VJ 
.. ~"-, t L~ 
Diane M. Griffiths 
/ 
General Counsel

sections.question_synthetic: May Councilmember David Malcolm participate in City Council decisions affecting a general partner in a business in which Malcolm is a limited partner, and may he vote on unrelated projects proposed by his partner?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC referenced its prior Nord Opinion (83-004) and newly adopted Regulation 18236 defining "otherwise related business entity" as the applicable framework for determining Malcolm's conflict of interest obligations regarding limited partnership interests and related business decisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a compilation of multiple related letters spanning July-December 1986. The primary FPPC response (December 16, 1986) provides synthetic conclusion but no formal Q/A sections. Initial request was withdrawn (August 13) then reinstated (November 14). Some source documents have OCR quality issues affecting readability.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May Councilmember David Malcolm participate in City Council decisions affecting a general partner in a business in which Malcolm is a limited partner, and may he vote on unrelated projects proposed by his partner?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC referenced its prior Nord Opinion (83-004) and newly adopted Regulation 18236 defining "otherwise related business entity" as the applicable framework for determining Malcolm's conflict of interest obligations regarding limited partnership interests and related business decisions.

embedding.summary: This file contains multiple 1986 FPPC documents regarding Councilmember David Malcolm's conflict of interest duties related to his limited partnership interests. The correspondence includes an initial request, withdrawal, reinstatement request, and final FPPC advice letter referencing the Nord Opinion and Regulation 18236.
